sutta: buddha said monks these 8 worldly conditions obsess the world the world revolves around these 8 worldly conditions 1 a gain and loss fame and dishonor praise and blame happiness and suffering conditions roka dhamma and chinese they have in the chinese buddhism they are called the 8 winds 8 winds which move a person will shake the person [Music] and then the buddha continued monks to the his in their hearts and the buddha said then listen monks give heed i will speak yes lord they replied and the exalted 1 said monks gain comes to the unlearned common average folk who reflect not thus are impermanent painful and subject to change nor do they know these conditions as they really are gain loss and so forth take possession of their minds and wholes way there they welcome the gain commentary: so here these 8 conditions they all come to us we all experience them but an ordinary person when he experiences these things he's moved by them when what he considers is good now like gain comes he's very happy but when loss comes he's very sad and these things gain loss etc take possession of of his mind and moves his mind very much he's moved very much by likes and dislikes and and so because of that now you find this person most people ordinary people and they have this monkey mind who is always happy and sad and excited most of the time and the only way to overcome this like and dislike is in is is mentioned in the saka pandha sutra in the deega nikaya where sakha devaraja asked the buddha what is the cause of people quarreling and fighting in this world and the buddha said in gree and jealousy and then raja asked what is the cause of greed and jealousy and the buddha said i think i can dislike learning and the cause of life and dislike is desire and the cause of desire is thinking and the cause of thinking is proliferation of thoughts and how to stop the proliferation of thoughts and then the buddha said there's 2 types of happiness 1 is that 1 type of happiness is the happiness of thinking another type of happiness is the happiness that comes from not thinking and the the happiness that comes from not thinking is the happiness that is that comes from the jhana janik bliss when the person attains the second jhana upwards the mind stops thinking and that kind of happiness is superior to the normal happiness that comes from thinking people get a lot of happiness from thinking that's why some people like to day dream they like to fantasize uh they they they like to imagine and there's only a type of happiness which which is higher than this which is charming bliss and so the way to overcome these likes and dislikes is to attain the bliss of jhana so that then we can overcome the likes and dislikes the next utah is

to time his own faults well it is for him to reveal the faults of another well it is to review his own attainments well it is to review another's now at that time the royal deva where savannah was on his way from the northern region to the south on some business or other and he heard the verbal uttara teaching the monks this doctrine in dava jalika then just as a strong man might stretch forth his bent arm or might bend his outstretched arm even so the royal deva savannah disappeared from dava jalika and appeared among the devas of the 33 and v savannah approached sakha devaraja and said your grace i would have you know that this verbal uttara in [Music] teaches this doctrine well it is from time to time to review 1's own faults well it is to review another's well it is to review 1's own attainments well it is to review another's then as a strong man might bend and stretch out his arm again saka king of the devas disappeared from among the devas of the 33 and appeared before the verbal uttara in dava jalika and sakha the deva king approached the venerable uttara and saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said is it true sir as they say that the venerable uttara teaches this doctrine to the monks namely well it is from time to time to review 1's own for to review anothers to review 1 owns attainments to reveal another's yes certainly they were king but pray sir is this the verbal uttara's own saying or is it the word of that exalted 1 arahan buddha now deva king i will give you an analogy for it is by analogy that men of intelligence understand the meaning of what he said imagine o king a great heap of grain near some village or market town from which country folk carry away corn on bingos or in baskets in lab or hand and if 1 should approach the fruit and question them saying from where i bring you this corn how would those folk in explaining best explain they would best explain the matter sir by saying we bring it from that great heap of grain even so o king whatsoever be well spoken all that is the word of the exalted 1 arahan buddha holy base thereon is both what me and others say it is marvelous it is wonderful sir how truly this items: Well it is for a monk to review from time to time his own false
Well it is for a monk to review from time to time another's false
Well it is for a month to review from time to time his own attainments relatives
Well it is to review from time to time another's attainments
Monks well it is that a monk should live mastering gain loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise
When a man lives with gain love's fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions and evil friendship which arise unmastered there arise the ass of us full of distress and anguish
But when those states are mastered those are full of distress and anguish are not for this good purpose
Therefore monks train yourselves thus we will live mastering gain which arises mastering loss fame obscurity honor lack of honor evil intentions mastering evil friendship which arises

sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 8 stains what a monks non-repeating is the mantra stain not rising is the stain of houses sluggishness is the stain of beauty carelessness is the stain of a god misconduct is the stain of a woman stint or restriction or limitation is the stain in giving evil and unwholesome states are stains in this world and the next but there is a greater stain than these ignorance is the greatest stain monks these are the 8 stains it's the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this utah we find that different blemishes or stains of different things but the greatest stain or blemish in the world his ignorance because of ignorance we do a lot of unwholesome evil deeds and cause the suffering of ourselves and of others therefore because of that ignorance is the greatest thing the next few times

sutta: monks a man enslaves a woman in 8 ways 1 a man enslaves a woman by appearance by laughter by speech by song by tears by attire or dress by garlands from the forest and by touch monks in these 8 ways a man enslaves a woman and being scorned by these are verily called as though in a snare that's the end of the suta
commentary: so these 2 sutas are a bit similar to the first suta the first 2 sutas we heard now in the angutaranika where the buddha said that there is no form that enslaves a man so much as the form of a woman no sound no smell no taste no touch that enslaves a man so much as the form a sound smell taste and touch of a woman and conversely the the verse is also true there is no uh form sounds smell taste and touch them and slaves uh woman so much as the form the sound the smell the taste and touch of a man so as we mentioned earlier the the opposite sex is a great obstacle to the spiritual path and these are the different ways in which this attraction of the opposite sex and attracts a person that is by appearance by laughter by speech universe by sound by song by tears by attire by garlands and by touch and the nexuta is quite an important and interesting suta

that the householder of asali is possessed of 8 wonderful and marvelous qualities that spoke the exalted 1 and thereafter rising from his seat the welfare entered the dwelling now a certain monk robbing himself early in the morning taking bowl and clone went to the house of ugga the householder and there sat down on a seat made ready for him [Music] and veysali approached that monk saluted him and sat down at 1 side then said that monk to ooga it has been declared by the exalted 1 that you householder are possessed of 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities what are those 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities householder of which you are declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed and uh said no reverend sir i know not of what 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities i am declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed but as to the 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities found in me listen lend an ear and i will tell you very well householder replied the monk and ooga said when i first saw the exalted 1 even a long way of my heart sir at the mere sight of him became tranquil this is the first marvelous and wonderful quality found in me then with tranquil heart i drew near and reverence the exalted 1 and to me he preached a graduated discourse that is to say on arms giving on virtue on heaven he set forth the peril the folly the depravity of lusts and the advantages of renunciation and when the exalted 1 knew that my heart was clear malleable free from hindrance uplifted lucid then he revealed to me the dhamma teaching to which buddhas alone have won namely bukka is arising its ending and the way to its ending just as a clean cloth free of all stain will die perfectly even so as i said there there are roles within me the spotless stainless dharma site that whatsoever is conditioned by coming to be all check to ending i sir saw dhamma possess dharma found dhamma penetrated dhamma overcame doubt surpassed uncertainty possessed without another's aid perfect confidence in the word of the teacher and there and then took refuge in the buddha in dhamma and in the sangha and embrace the 5 rules of training in the holy life this sir is the second quality [Music] now sir i had 4 wives young girls and i went and spoke to them thus sisters i have embraced the 5 rules of training in the holy life who wishes may enjoy the wealth of this place all may do deeds of merit or may go to her own relations and family or is there some man you desire to whom i may give you and when i had stopped speaking the elder's wife said to me sir give me to such and such a man then i had that man sent forth and taking my wife by the left hand and holding the water pot in my right i cleansed that man by an act of dedication yet i was not aware and discomforted at parting with my wife this is the third quality moreover sir there is wealth in my family but among the good and lovely in character it is shared impartially this is the fourth quality and when i read a point amongst her i serve him respectfully and not without deference that is without respect this sir is the fifth quality hipster the reverend monk preached dhamma to me i listened attentively and not listlessly if he please not dhamma to me i preach dharma to him this sir is the sixth quality furthermore sir it is not uncommon for devas to come and declare to me perfectly o householder is dhamma proclaimed by the exalted 1 when they speak to us i reply you devas whether you declare so or not dhamma is perfectly proclaimed by the exalted 1 yes i feel no elation because of such thoughts as it is to me these devas come i am the man who talks to devas this sir is the seventh quality of those 5 lower factors declared by the exalted 1 i see no remnant whatsoever within me this sir is the eighth marvelous and wonderful quality found in me these are the 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities found in me but i do not know of 1 aid i am declared by the exalted 1 to be possessed then that monk after taking arms at ugga's house roars on his seat and departed and when he had eaten his meal after his arms round he went to the exalted 1 and saluting him stood at 1 side so standing he told the exalted 1 of all his conversations with ooga the householder of the asali and the exalted 1 said well done well done monk as ugga the householder of asali in explaining the matter rightly should explain even so monks he is declared by me to be possessed of those 8 marvelous and wonderful qualities moreover monk wrote it true that the householder of isali is so possessed that's the end of the sutta

sutta: is quite a good suta once the exalted 1 dwelt among the sakyans in banyan tree at kapilavatu there mahanama the sakyan came and visited him and after saluting sat down at 1 side there addressing the exalted 1 he said lord how becomes a man a lay disciple and the buddha said when mahanama he has found refuge in the buddha found refuge in the dhamma found refuge in the sangha then he is a lay disciple lord how is a lay disciple virtuous when mahanama a lay disciple abstains from taking life abstains from taking what is not given to him abstains from lustful and evil conduct abstains from lying and abstains from spirits and intoxicants the cause of indolence then a lay disciple is virtuous lord how does a lay disciple help on his own welfare but not that of another when mahanama he has achieved faith for himself but strives not to compass faith in another has achieved virtue for self but strives not to compass virtue in another has achieved himself renunciation but strives not to compass renunciation in another longs himself to see the monks but strives not for this side for another longs himself to hear such dharma or true dhamma but strives not for this hearing for another is mindful himself of dhamma he has heard but strives not that another should be mindful of it reflects himself upon the meaning of dhamma he is mindful of but strives not for another to reflect their own but he knows himself both the letter and the spirit of dhamma and walks in conformity there with but strives not for another soul to walk then a lay disciple helps on his own welfare but not that of another and how lord does a lay disciple help on both his own welfare and the welfare of another when indeed mahanama he has achieved faith himself and strives to compose faith in another as as he has achieved virtue himself and strives to compass virtue in another renunciation longs to see the monks to hear saddam his mindful reflex but he knows both the letter and the spirit of dhamma and walks in conformity there with and strives to make another sort of wrong then mahanama a lay disciple helps on his own welfare and the welfare of another it's the end of the sutta
commentary:
this suta is quite important because here it gives a definition of a lay disciple a lay disciple is 1 who has taken refuge in the buddha dhamma and sangha and you just have to take refuge on your own and you are already considered a lay disciple it's only nowadays sometimes there is a ceremony but there don't need to be a ceremony if you on your own in your own heart take refuge to the buddha dharma and the sangha then you are already a lay disciple and a lay disciple is virtuous by keeping the 5 precepts so the 5 precepts are the practice of a lay disciple and then the lady disciples helps himself if he achieves these few things he has faith has virtue practices renunciation he hears the true dhamma saddam which means the discourses the original discourses of the buddha and he is mindful of dhamma he reflects upon the meaning of dharma that he has heard and then knowing both the letter and the spirit of dhamma he walks in conformity there with and he practices all these things then he is helping himself but if he practices these things and tries to help others to practice these things then he not only benefits himself but benefits others that's why in 1 of the sutas the buddha said that those people that you love those who are near and dear to you especially your family members it is good to bring them to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha that way you will benefit them a lot and also if you hope to meet them in the future life it's also important to bring them to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha because the buddha said if 2 persons are to meet together in the next life 4 things should be the same 1 is faith another 1 is conduct moral that means equally virtuous or equally evil and the third 1 is charity equally generous or equally selfish and the fourth 1 is wisdom equally wise or equally foolish so if we bring our near and dear relatives and friends to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha and practice like we practice and then the chances of meeting them together in the next life would be better

sutta: the buddha said monks there are these 8 years in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which are conducive to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness 1 aid here in monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in the buddha this monks is the first yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness etc again monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in dhamma this is the second eu in merit and goodness again monks an aryan disciple has found refuge in the sangha this is the third yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which conduces to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness monks there are these 5 gifts great gifts recognized from the first known for many a day known by tradition ancient and unconfounded not being confounded in the past they have not become confounded nor will they become confounded they are not scorned by discerning recluse or brahmins what 5 herein monks an aryan disciple abandons taking life and abstains there from thus abstaining to unnumbered beings he gives fearlessness he gives non-hatred he gives non-ill will and in giving fearlessness non-hatred non-ill will he becomes a partaker in unbounded fearlessness enmity and goodwill this month is the first gift and scorned by discerning recluse and brahmanas this monks is the fourth yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness etc again monks and aryan disciples abandon stealing and abandons all lustful evil ways abandons lying abandons the use of intoxicants which cause indolence and abstains therefrom this monks is the fifth gift a gift a great gift recognized from the first known for many a day known by tradition ancient and unconfounded not being confounded in the past it has not become confounded nor will it become confounded it is not scorned by discerning recluse of brahmin this monks is the eighth yield in merit and goodness the food of happiness celestial resulting in happiness leading heavenward which conduces to what is pleasing lovely precious beneficial and to happiness amongst these are the heals in merit and goodness as the end of the sutta

sutta: [Music] the buddha mounts taking life when pursued practiced increased brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm what is the very trifling result of taking life is the shortening of a man's life monks stealing when pursued brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very trifling result is man's loss of wealth monks fleshly lusts when pursued brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very trifling result is a man's rivalry and hatred monks lying when pursued brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very trifling result is the slandering and false speaking of a man monks malicious tale bearing when pursuit brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very tribling result is the breaking up of a man's friendship monk's harsh speech when pursued brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very trifling result is an unpleasing noise for a man monks idle talk when pursued brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm the very trifling result is unacceptable speech for a man monks drinking strong drink when pursued practice increased brings 1 to hell to an animal's womb to the ghost realm what is a very trifling result of drinking strong drink is madness for a man that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here normally concerning the precepts we hear about the 5 precepts but we notice in the sutasa that sometimes the buddha mentions 7 precepts and in this uttar please mention the 7 precepts plus the liquor precept which makes it the 8 precepts and it seems from the sutasa that originally the buddha made 7 precepts the first 1 is to abstain from killing second to abstain from taking what is not given the third is abstain from adultery the fourth is to abstain from lying [Music] and [Music] another 3 are also connected with verbal karma the fifth is abstention from malicious steel bearing that means not to when you hear a talking bad about b you don't repeat to be what a said to make them moral and fighting that is abstention from malicious tear bearing and sixth 1 is harsh speech abstention from heart speech the seventh is abstention from idol tom and these were the original 7 precepts and and they uh if you look at the iron eightfold path and they are found there and the fifth precept the the the precept against ring was added later and it is also not found in the ariana eightfold path so you notice in these 8 precepts uh if you break these a precepts constantly and then we'll be reborn into the ghost realm but even if we don't make a habit of breaking these 8 precepts these precepts if we uh if we do break them the first 1 the result of killing taking life the trifling result is the shortening of a man's life you'll have short life because you make other beings life short the second 1 stealing the trifling result of stealing now is a man's loss of wealth that means you will also your wealth will also be stolen and then adultery trifling result of adultery results in rivalry and hatred in the next life and lying and the result of lying the trifling result of lying is slandering and false speaking of that person in return and then malicious tale bearing the result is breaking up of a man's friendship because you break up other person's friendships so you will also suffer the same result then harsh feature the result noise idle talk the result is unacceptable speech in the future and taking liquor and intoxicants and the trifling result of that now is madness in the future life so because of that now we have to be careful to keep these precepts purely the nexuta is

sutta: once the exalted 1 was dwelling at gosambi in gosita park now the venerable anuruddha had gone apart during the known day rest and there there came to him a host of lovely davies of aries who saluted and stood at 1 side thus standing they addressed the vulnerable 1 and said master anuruda we are the fairies of lovely form in 3 degrees reveal power and have dominion we can assume in a moment any color we desire we can produce in a moment any sound we desire we can obtain in a moment any happiness we desire we master anuruddha are the fairies of lovely form and in these 3 degrees we have power and dominion then the verbal anuruda thought oh that these fairies would become all blue with blue faces blue garments and blue finery and those fairies knowing his thoughts became all blue with blue faces blue garments and blue finery then he thought oh that they would become all yellow all red all white with white faces white garments and white finery and at each thought they became so now some of the fairies sang some danced and some clapped their hands this is the 5 instruments of music when well tuned well played and properly struck by the skilled heal the sound at 1 sweet and charming alluring lovable and bewitching even so was the music of those fairies all bedecked at once sweet and charming alluring lovable and bewitching but the verbal anuruda kept his senses under control then taught the fairies master anuruddha is not enjoying this and immediately vanished in the evening the venerable anuruda arose from solitude and came where the exhorted 1 was and after saluting he sat down at 1 side so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had happened adding lord how many qualities have women who on the breaking up of the body after death are reborn among the fairies of lovely form 8 qualities have women on ruda who after death are reborn among the fairies of lovely form 1 8 here in arudha the husband to whom her parents give her out of love for her seeking her good in loving kindness and foreign regard she will get up before him retire after him be obedient to his wishes lovely in her ways and gentle in speech whosoever is honored by her husband as mother father recluse of brahmin such she reverences honors esteems and respects on their arrival she offers the seed and water whatever her husband's home industries whether in wool cotton or cotton therein she is deft and nimble gifted with an inquiring turn of mind into all such undertakings she is able to arrange and carry them out of whatever her husband's household consists slaves messengers are workful she knows the work of each by what has been done she knows their remissness by what has not been done she knows the strength and the weakness of the sick she divides the hard and soft food each according to his share when her husband brings home money corn silver or gold she keeps it secure by watch and ward of it she is no robber thief squanderer waster she is a lay disciple who has found refuge in the buddha in dhamma and sangha she keeps the precepts abstaining from taking life from stealing from especially lusts from a duck from lying from drinking strong during the clock the cause of sloth she is charitable dwelling at home with heart burst of the stain of stinginess given over to charity open handed taking delight in giving you made to asking she finds joy in arms giving these are the 8 qualities women have who after death are reborn among the fairies of lovely form that's the end of the sutta
commentary: it's quite an interesting suta because the parable anuruddha he went to the forest after taking his meal the middle of the day i went into the forest and in the forest when a monk is alone sometimes these spirits come to him because he's all alone so these days fairies came to him and showed their ability to change their colors and to play music et cetera and but senses and uh controls were the fairies left and then the when consulted the buddha the buddha said now that a woman who is born as this type of area of lovely form she has 8 qualities now she serves her husband secondly she honors her husband's parents and monks third she's capable of managing the husband's business the fourth is capable in running the household the fifth regards the husband's wealth 6 she takes refuge in the buddha dhamma and sangha [Music] the seventh she keeps the 5 precepts and the number 8 she's generous or charitable so that's the way to become a fairy of lovely former for a woman the next suta is

sutta: is quite an interesting sutta once while the exalted 1 was staying among the sakians in banyan tree park at kapilavatu maha pajapati came and visited him and after saluting stood at 1 side thus standing she said to the exalted 1 lord well worried that women should be allowed to go forth from the home to homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tatargata [Music] and the buddha said enough gothamite set not your heart upon the going forth of women from the home to the homeless life into the dhammavinaya declared by the tata gather but mahapadjapati besieged him a second time and a third time in light manner but the exalted 1 replied as before then mahapadjapati the gotham seeing that the exalted 1 would not permit women to go forth from the home sad and sorrowful in tears and sobbing saluted him and departed keeping him on her right just stop here to comment this was actually the master mother of our buddha when our buddha was young according to the legend 7 days after our buddha was born the real mother passed away and then the mother's sister is this mahabhaja party and maha bhajapati was also the wife of the buddha's father in the in the olden days a person could have as many wives as he could afford to keep that so um so in actual fact mahaprabhu was the anti of the buddha and mahabapati had her own son by the name of nanda and as she brought up nanda she also brought up and nursed our buddha just like her own son and when the buddha had become enlightened and came back to see the the sakyans and taught them the dhamma and at that time this mahapadhapati was already very old so she wanted to become a nun and she was the first to ask the buddha for permission to ordain but as you see here the buddha refused her permission now this term continues now the exalted 1 when he had stayed at kapilavatu as long as he desired set out on a journey to visali and in due course going from place to place arrived there and the exalted 1 dwelt near vesali in mahavana at the gabled hall then maha pajapati the gothamine having had her hair cut off and dawn yellow robes set out with a large company of sakian women for vasali and in due course they drew near to the gable hall in mahavana near vesali and maha pajapati stood outside the door her feet swollen and her limbs covered with dust sad and sorrowful sobbing and in tears now the wemble ananda saw her standing down thus with swollen feet and in tears and said to her wherefore gautamin do you stand sad and sorrowful outside the door and she said it is because reverend ananda the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tata gather and verbal ananda said then wait here go to it until i have asked the exalted 1 to allow women to go forth from the home and the verbal ananda went unto the exalted 1 and saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he spoke thus lord mahaprabhu stands outside at the door her feet swollen and her limbs covered with dust sad and sorrowful sobbing and in tears saying the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth lord well worried that women should be allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless light into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tata gata and the buddha said enough ananda set not your heart upon the going forth of women from the home to the homeless life the second and the third time ananda beseeched him in like manner but the exalted 1 gave the same reply then taught the verb the exalted 1 will not allow women to go forth from the home to the homeless life what if i were to ask the exalted 1 in another way and he spoke thus lord if women go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya and declared by the tata gata can they realize the fruit of stream winning of 1's returning of non-returning and of arahanship [Music] and the buddha said they can [Music] lord if they can since mahaprabhu has been of great service to the exalted 1 for as his aunt nurse and foster mother she gave him milk and the exalted 1's mother died well worried that women should be allowed to go forth and the buddha said if ananda mahaprabhu received these 8 cardinal rules it shall be for her the ordination 1 though she has been ordained a 100 years a nun must pay respect raise her hands in salutation rise up from her seat and salute a monk who has but that day been ordained this is a rule to be honored respected revered venerated and never to be transgressed during her life 2 um none must not spend the rainy season where there is no resident monk this is a rule never to be transgressed 3 twice a month a nun must question the sangha concerning the date of the upusvata day and the next time of preaching this is a rule never to be transgressed for after the reigning seas rainy season a nun must keep the pavarana before both sanghas in respect of 3 matters those seen those heard and those suspected this is a rule never to be transgressed [Music] 5 a none guilty of a serious offense must undergo a penance before both sanghas for the half month this is a rule never to be transgressed 6 after training for 2 rainy seasons in the 6 rules and none must seek full ordination of both sanghas this is a rule never to be transgressed 7 not on any pretext must none revile or abuse among this is a rule never to be transgressed a from today ananda admonition by nuns of monks is forbidden but admonition of nuns by monks is not forbidden this is a rule to be honored respected revered venerated and never to be transgressed during life if maha pajapati received these 8 cardinal rules it shall be for her the ordination now when the verbal ananda had learned these 8 rules from the exalted 1 he went to mahapantapati and told her all the exalted 1 had said and she answered him and said ananda just as a woman or man young and tender in years and fawn of dress would after washing the head received with both hands a garland of lotus flowers of jasmine flowers or of some sweet scented creeper and place it on top of the head even so eyes receive these 8 cardinal rules never to be transgressed all my life then the venable ananda returned to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said lord maha bhajapati the gothamit has received the 8 rules and the buddha said if ananda women had not been allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless life into the dhamma vinaya declared by the tathagata then long would have lasted the holy life for a thousand years would have said dhamma or true dharma would sadama or true dhamma have lasted but now ananda since women have gone forth not for long will the holy life last now and under just for 500 years will the true dharma last just as those clans that have many women and but few men easily fall a prey to robbers and potties even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth from the home to the homeless life that holy life will not last long just as when the disease known as white as bones falls upon the field of ripened rice that field does not last long even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth that holy life will not last long just as when the disease known as red rust falls upon a field of ripened sugarcane that field does not last long even so ananda in whatever dhamma vinaya women are allowed to go forth that holy life will not last long and now ananda just as a man might build a dive to a great reservoir with a view to the future so that no water could pass beyond it even so i ananda with a view to the future have laid down for nuns these 8 cardinal rules which must not be transgressed so long as they live that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so we see from here after mahamati could not get the permission of the buddha to go forth and then he followed the buddha all the way here walking from kapilava to 2 ways ali and according to the commentary and there was a distance of about 200 miles so that was a pretty long way to walk and [Music] so the venable ananda asked the buddha again for permission for women to go forth and 3 times the buddha refused him and then after that he thought of another way to get rounded now and reminded the the buddha that the buddha owed very much to mahaprabhu who brought her up and gave her milk when she was small etc and so the buddha relented and the buddha made 8 rules for nuns the first 1 is that even a nun has been ordained a long time she must be respect to a monk any monk even though he is very newly ordained then second rule is that monk must not spend the rainy season davasa where there is no resident monk in the theravada tradition every year during the rainy season in india they had the rainy season the winter and the summer seasons are 3 seasons and 3 months out of the 4 months a month or a none must stay in a fixed place and not leave the place more than 6 continuous nights so for a nanna she must spend the vasa in a place where there are monks at least 1 monk and the third condition is that twice a month a nun must ask the bhikkhu sangha for the date of the uposata because i think they have to recite the precepts in front of the monk and then the time of preaching because every 2 weeks they would have to be given a teaching by a senior monk and the fourth rule is after the rainy season a nun must keep the pavarana before both sanghas pavarana is an invitation to criticize it is also practiced by mangala when monks has been the vasa the rainy season and after the 3 months of the rainy season sometimes there is some ill will between monks so they have to each 1 of them they have to in a sangha gathering starting from the most senior manga they have to invite criticism from the other monks invite the monks to criticize them not in case the any of the monks has seen or heard or suspected anything wrong then the monk invites criticism so for a case in the case of a nun she has to invite the nuns to criticize her and later she has to ask the monks to invite the monks to criticize her as well the fifth ruler is she has undergone a serious offense meaning a sangha disease the second most serious offense for a manga nanna is a sangha disease for example to touch somebody of the opposite sex purposely uh out of a lustful desire or to say some words some vulgar words etc so for this class of serious offenses she and none must undergo a penance before both sanghas the monks and the nuns the sixth rule after training for 2 rainy seasons that means for 2 years in the 6 rules a nun she cannot immediately become a nun she has to keep 6 precepts for 2 years that was partly because once there was a nun who came to ordain and after she was ordained then she became pregnant and that was not because she did anything wrong but because she was married before she ordained so after she ordained she conceived and then people talked bad about her so this was probably the main reason why the buddha made a ruler that none must spend 2 years before she gets the full ordination and then the seventh rule is that a nun must not revile or abuse among and the eighth rule is that monks can criticize nuns but nuns are not allowed to criticize monks now sometimes women are not happy with this kind of discrimination against women but it's partly because women are more emotional than men so that if they are kind then they are kinder than men but if they are angry or hateful then they become much more hateful than men so that's for this reason and um towards the end the buddha told ananda that if women had not been allowed to go forth and then the true dharma would have lasted 1 000 years but because women had gone forth then the true dharma would now last only 500 years and so we can see that the the dhamma was unadulterated for 500 years and after 500 years after after the buddhas passing away from that time onwards new books were written new books that contradicted the earlier discourses of the buddha so that if we study the sutasa we find the earliest 4 nikayas the diga nikaya majima nikaya sangyuta nikaya and anguttara nikaya these are the earliest discourses of the buddha these if you read the sutasa there are no contradictions generally but if you read later books and then you find that there's control over the books and then the new newer books they contradict each other as well so we have to be very careful now that the dhamma has become polluted it doesn't mean that the the true dharma is lost okay the true dharma is still here but it is um mixed up with a lot of our dhamma what is contrary to dhamma so it is very difficult for people to distinguish between the real dhamma and the fake dhamma and so [Music] if you don't get the proper guidance because most people don't have the time to study all the books to find out then you might get some wrong views so you see and the reason the buddha at first refused permission for women to go forth was actually to protect the dhamma he wanted the dhamma to pro to last longer to benefit more people and he said once women have gone forth then the sasana the the buddhist religion has become weakened the teachings have become weakened and the buddha gave an example those clans and they have many women but few men easy for easily fall prey to robbers and pot thieves so in the same way [Music] once you have women in the holy life and then the sasana is weakened

sutta: once while the exalted 1 was staying at the gable hall in mahavana near vaisali the weber ananda came to him and saluted and sat down at 1 side associated he spoke thus to the exalted 1 lord how many qualities must a man have to be considered a spiritual advisor of nuns among mananda must have 8 qualities among is virtuous he undertakes and trains himself in the precepts he is learning the doctrines he has fully understood in theory to whom both party morka have been properly and fully handed down sectioned regulated and resolved into detail he has a pleasant voice his enunciation is good his speech is obeying distinct free from hoarseness and informative he is able to instruct inside rouse and gladden the sangha of nuns with religious discourse generally he is dear to and liked by the nuns previous to his taking this exalted 1 as his authority for going forth for donning the yellow robe he has been guilty of no serious crime he has been ordained 20 years or more among ananda must have these 8 qualities to be considered a spiritual advisor for nuns that's the end of the sutta
commentary: i just recapitulate these conditions and the first 1 among must be virtuous he keeps the precepts a second secondly he must be learned in the dhamma thirdly he the both parties the monks patimoka and the nuns patimoca he fully understands number 4 he has a pleasant voice number 5 he's able to instruct the nuns with religious discourse with the dhamma number 6 he is liked by the nuns number 7 he has been guilty of no serious crime number 8 he has been ordained 20 years or more among what's been ordained 10 years or more is called a terror monk who has been ordained 20 years or more is called a maha thera so these are the qualities the nexus is

sutta: once in the gable hall at veithali maha pajapati the gotami spoke thus to the exalted 1 well word for me lord if the exalted 1 would teach me dhamma briefly so that after hearing the exalted 1's word i might dwell alone secluded earnest zealous and resolute and the buddha said those things of which you know thus these things lead to passion not to release there from these to lead to bondage not to release there from these lead to the piling of rebirth not to the dispersion thereof these lead to wanting much not wanting little these lead to discontent not to contentment these lead to sociability not to solitude these lead to indolence not to exertion these lead to luxury not to frugality of these things oh definitely this is not dhamma this is not vinaya this is not the word of the teacher but as to those things gotami which you know lead to this passion to release from bondage to the dispersion of rebirth to wanting little to contentment to solitude to exertion and to frugality and in no case to the opposites be assured that they are dhamma they are vinaya the word of the teacher that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the buddha is giving a dharma teaching in brief to the foster mother to the mother and uh these are the things that uh there is dharma in a concise form that those things that lead to dispassion to release from bondage to dispersion of rebirth to wanting little to contentment to solitude to exertion to frugality these are consistent with the dhamma vinaya but not their opposite the nexus

sutta: the buddha said monks a man is endowed with 8 qualities among who is endowed with 8 qualities is worthy of offerings worthy of gifts worthy of oblations deserves to be reverently saluted the world's peerless field for merit what a hearing among is virtuous he is learned number 2 number 3 he dwells strenuous steadfast energetic shirking not the burden of righteousness or wholesomeness number 4 he is a forest dweller having his bed and seed apart from mankind number 5 enduring likes and dislikes dwelling in continuous mastery of dislikes which arise number 6 enduring fear and dismay dwelling in continuous mastery of fear and dismay which arise number 7 he attains to the 4 jana states number 8 he destroys the asavas he who is endowed with these 8 qualities is worthy of offerings worthy of gifts worthy of oblations deserves to be reverently saluted the world's fearless field for merit that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
so these are the 8 qualities which makes the monk very virtuous and worthy of uh salutations number 1 is virtuous number 2 his learner that means he's learned in the dhamma the buddha's teachings uh more specifically the dhamma vinaya the sutas and the vinaya and then number 3 he is energetic always getting rid of unwholesome states of mind and developing wholesome states of mind number 4 he is a forest dweller that means he dwells in seclusion having his lodgings away from mankind number 5 is always [Music] trying to master dislikes which arise and number 6 is always trying to master fear which arise fear which arise is more likely in lonely places if among dwells alone in lonely places and sometimes at night [Music] there might be certain things certain sounds which make him afraid so he has to master the fear number 7 he attains to the 4 janas the 4 janas the jhanas are considered uttari manosa dhamma higher than human attainment that is why the person can attain the janus he is worthy of um reverence and the last 1 he destroys the asaba becomes liberated

sutta: monks there are these 8 persons worthy of offerings worthy of gifts worthy of oblations deserving to be reverently saluted the world's fearless field for married 1 8 the stream winner that's number 1 number 2 he who has entered the path for the realization of the fruit of stream winning number 3 the once returner or sakadagami number 4 he who attains to the path for the realization thereof number 5 the non-returner or anagamin number 6 he who attains he who has entered the path for the realization of the fruit of non-returning i number 7 the arahant and number 8 he was entered on the path to arahantship that's the end of the
commentary: so here the buddha is talking about 8 persons who are aryas and you notice it is 8 persons not 4 persons because in the abi dharma the path attainment is supposed to be followed immediately the next moment by the fruition but this is not so in the sutas in the sutas you have uh 8 persons which means that a path attainer actually exists for you to make off rings too so it does appear in the success that path attainment and fruition attainment can take quite some time their partner quite some time apart not necessarily 1 following the other immediately the other thing you notice is that these terms sutapanas are all fruition attainers they're all fruition attainers here in this book they have translated wrongly they have translated the stream winner and he who attains to the realization of the fruit of stream winning so from this translation it appears like the stream winner sutapana

sutta: monks there are these 8 assemblies 1 8 assemblies of nobles or brahmins of householders of recluses of devas of the 4 royal devas of the 33 devas of maharas and of brahmas now monks i call to mind having visited many hundreds of times an assembly of nobles of brahmins of householders of recluses of the 4 royal devas of deva supper amaras of brahmas and before even i had seated myself among them or had spoken to them or had engaged them in conversation whatever their color that i became whatever their language that became mine and i instructed them incited them to rouse them and gladden them with dhamma discourse and they knew me not when i spoke but reasoned among themselves saying who is this who speaks man or deva then when i instructed incited raos and gladdened them with dhamma discourse i vanished and they knew me not when i was gone but questioned each other who is this who has vanished man or deva monks these are the 8 assemblies that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this is another interesting suta where the buddha shows that he uses psychic power to visit various types of devas and men and he assumed their form and he spoke their language and he gave them dharma teachings and then after that he just vanished and they were surprised who when they were they're wondering who he was and he just disappeared the next utah is

sutta: months when mindfulness and awareness are lacking conscientiousness and sense of remorse are caused to be destroyed when conscientiousness and sense of remorse are lacking the control of the senses is caused to be destroyed but the control of the senses is lacking moral practice sila is caused to be destroyed when moral practice is lacking right concentration is caused to be destroyed when right concentration is lacking seeing and knowing of things as they really are are caused to be destroyed and seeing and knowing of things as they really are are lacking weariness and dispassion are caused to be destroyed when weariness and dispassion are lacking seeing and knowing of liberation are caused to be destroyed monks just as in the case of a tree which is devoid of branches and foliage and the shoots thereof come not to maturity nor the bark nor the sap would nor the core even soul monks when mindfulness and awareness are lacking conscientiousness and sense of remorse are caused to be destroyed when conscientiousness and sense of remorse are lacking control of the senses are caused to be is caused to be destroyed etc etc monks when mindfulness and awareness are present conscientiousness and sense of remorse are the are caused to be complete when conscientiousness and sense of remorse are present the control of the senses is caused to be complete but the control of the senses is present moral practice is caused to be complete when moral practice is present right concentration is caused to be complete when right concentration is present seeing and knowing of things as they really are is caused to be complete when seeing and knowing of things as they really are are present weariness and dispassion are caused to be complete but weariness and dispassion are present seeing and knowing liberation are caused to be complete monks just as in the case of a tree possessing branches and foliage the shoots thereof come to maturity so likewise the bark the sap wood and the core even so amongst when mindfulness and awareness are present conscientiousness and sense of remorse are caused to be complete etc etc that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so here you can see here liberation comes from several causes and conditions and all these have to be present and they are very systematic 1 leads to another you see mindfulness and awareness leads to conscientiousness and sense of remorse and conscientious and sense of remorse leads to control of the senses and control of the senses leads to moral practice and moral practice leads to right concentration which leads to seeing and knowing of things as they really are and that leads to weariness and dispassion and weariness and dispassion leads to seeing and knowing liberation [Music] liberation itself now of all these things uh you uh the important point to note some of the important points to note is that moral practice leads to right concentration without sila precepts it's very difficult to get right concentration because the mind is disturbed with remorse a person keeps silla moral practice he has no remorse to disturb his mind so he easily gets into right concentration and right concentration leads to seeing knowing seeing and knowing things as they really are this is an important point that is the purpose of right concentration samadhi strong concentration is to get rid of the hindrances so that we can see things as they really are without strong concentration it is impossible not to see things as they really are we only see things as they seem to us and then seeing and knowing things as they really are leads to weariness and dispassion weariness is nibida i think and this passion is with raga and when we are weary of the things in this world we become dispassionate towards them no more it is no more the passion for worldly things and that leads to liberation now we come to suta number

sutta: now the verbal punia paid a visit to the exhorted 1 and sat down at 1 side and said lord what is the cause what is the reason why sometimes it occurs to the tatargata to preach dhamma and sometimes it does not suppose punya a monk is a believer but does not visit him then it does not occur to the tatargata to preach dhamma but when among is a believer and visits the tatargata then it occurs to him to preach dharma or among is a believer and visits the tatargata but does not sit down to listen then it does not occur to the tatargata to preach dharma or he sits down to listen but does not question that or he questions the tata gata but does not listen to dhamma with attentive ear or he listens with attentive ear but when he has heard it does not bear dhamma in mind or he bears what he has heard in mind but does not examine the truth of the doctrines he has heard or he examines the truth of the doctrines but walks not in conformity with dhamma though knowing both letter and spirit then it does not occur to the tatar gather to preach dhamma but when punia among is a believer and visits the tita sits down to listen questions the tata gaata listens with attentive ear bears what he has heard in mind examines the truth of the doctrines heard and knowing both the letter and the spirit walks in conformity with dhamma then it occurs to the tata to preach dhamma and these 8 conditions come to pass punya then it most occurs to the tata to preach dhamma that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so from this we find that sometimes people come to visit the buddha sometimes he preaches dharma to them and sometimes he does not and these are the reasons he give sometimes that person is not inclined to listen or sometimes they listen but does not question the buddha anymore or he questions but does not listen with attentive ear etc and the buddha [Music] the buddha taught now that it is we should have reverence of the dhamma and a lay person especially has to show respect and be desiring to hear the dhamma before the dhamma is taught then we find in the vinaya rules the buddha laid down that if early persons if a lay person sits higher than among he is not to be taught the dhamma or he wears a head or he claps the knees grasp the knees or he carries a weapon etc then the monk is not supposed to teach that person the dhamma in other words the dhamma is something that is invaluable and so we should show respect for it before it will be taught to us the nexus
